content.text: Everything on this page is bought to be burned. Budget accordingly.
The baseline consumable catalog. Systems is the tree that tunes these into something better (see Systems); the electronic-warfare set lives on Mods.

rows: Heal 1d6 HP. Stabilizes a Down ally with a Medicine Check — the stabilize window lives on The Staircase.
Field-restore 1d6 Armor HP. Baseline use risks the permanent-consequence roll; Shell Splint (Systems) and Warranty Work (Tinkerer) wave it, each in its lane. Field repair lives on Armor.
Shed Slowed; move 1 band free; +1 Charge. The body bills later (Charge lives on Charge & Saturation).
End Seared and toxin riders on one target.
Adjacent cluster at Medium range: 2d6, no attack roll.
One target or barrier: 2d8, punch-through doubled vs Armor HP. The poor man's Shatter Pulse.
The quiet line every sheet pays.

content.text: Nobody counts standard rounds. There is no per-shot tracking. Instead, each gun you used on a mission incurs a flat resupply cost at mission's end. Heavy-usage missions scale the bill up, at GM call.

Specialty rounds are tracked individually. Bought per round, declared when fired — you palm the round, 0 AP if it's prep-carried (prep slots live on Systems). Each one is a decision, which is exactly why they're the ones you count.

rows: Punch-through counts double vs Armor HP.
Even a graze applies Rattled (see Conditions).
Delivers one loaded consumable condition on a full hit.
Embeds a mark — counts as a Spotter mark (see Systems; the Tag-Sync Receiver mod extends it).

content.text: Precision is the tree that turns this catalog into a fire-control system — its augments live on Precision.
Half price — and a natural 1 on the accuracy die is a jam. The weapon is Jammed: it doesn't fire until cleared, 1 AP (see Conditions).
cheap rounds collect on a schedule of their own choosing.

Ammunition is licensed IP, and corp rounds are watermarked: spent brass is evidence. A Tinkerer's press and a Fixer's quiet suppliers are how you live outside that — the Tinkerer handloads your specialty rounds at material cost, and the Fixer tells you what to load before you know you need it (see Professions Overview).
Expended munitions remain corporate property. Please police your brass — forensic recovery is billed by the casing.
